heroHeadline: Somalia: Pirates Hijack Fuel Tanker Honour 25 with 17 Crew Off Puntland; Constitutional Deadline 20 Days Away; Adan Yabal Stalemate Enters Day 6
heroSubtitle: Somali pirates seized the Palau-flagged tanker Honour 25 and its 17-member multinational crew on April 25 — approximately 30 nautical miles offshore between Hafun and Bandarbeyla on the Puntland coast — as the vessel carried 18,500 barrels of fuel from Berbera to Mogadishu. EU NAVFOR Atalanta and Combined Task Force 151 deployed in response; no ransom demands issued in initial reporting. The hijacking follows a possible armed boarding investigated 24 NM off Xaafuun on April 22, confirming a sharpening piracy resurgence along Somalia's northeastern coast since October 2025 that now threatens the Berbera–Mogadishu coastal fuel supply route. Meanwhile, Somalia's constitutional crisis deepens as President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's term expires in 20 days (May 15), the Somali Future Council's Salvation Summit ultimatum remains active, and IGAD has issued an urgent call for 8,000 additional peacekeepers to address the surge in Al-Shabaab attacks. The SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19) enters Day 6 in the mobilization phase, with Al-Shabaab continuing to reinforce defensive positions and set IED belts on approach routes in Hiiraan Region.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, EU EPF, EU NAVFOR, Combined Maritime Forces, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
